\boxed{\text{The transition marks a shift from a small, mundane perspective (the ant) to a grand, mythic one (the centaur in a dragon world).}}

This line juxtaposes the **microcosmic** (the ant’s world) with the **macrocosmic** (mythological grandeur), creating a surreal, symbolic leap. The contrast highlights the poem’s theme of **perception and scale**, often seen in works like Wallace Stevens’ *"The Emperor of Ice-Cream"* or Sylvia Plath’s *"Daddy."* The answer reflects the most common critical interpretation of such a device.
